clinical_history: Patient reports right ankle pain and swelling after a fall during a rugby game. The foot turned inwards during the fall, and the pain is located at the lateral malleolus. The patient rates the pain as 7 or 8 out of 10 and has difficulty bearing weight on the affected ankle.

detailed_findings.explanation: The right ankle is swollen with a bruise approximately four centimeters wide.
Significant pain is noted on the lateral malleolus upon palpation.
The patient has difficulty pointing toes and foot upwards towards the face.
The patient experiences pain when pushing the foot down like pressing a gas pedal.
The patient has a limp and an inability to bear weight on the right ankle.

impression_summary: The clinical features suggest a possible ankle fracture or sprain, with significant tenderness on the lateral side and difficulty bearing weight.
recommendations: Obtain an x-ray to rule out a fracture.
Consider air boot or casting if a fracture is confirmed.
